ShortDescription.Value: Bring democracy to your city! Citizens propose projects, vote on initiatives and elect a mayor whose agenda shapes the city.

Publish.LongDescription:
Civic Voice adds a living governance layer to your city. Citizens submit proposals based on your city's real needs, vote in annual mayoral elections and hold you accountable for the state of your city.
Citizens submit proposals based on your city's real needs. High unemployment? Residents demand job creation. Housing shortage? Citizens call for new development. Proposals are driven by live city data so they always feel relevant to your current situation.
**Three Tiers of Projects**
* Urgent Issues — triggered automatically when city metrics fall below acceptable levels * Citizen Proposals — general improvements influenced by the current mayor's specialty * Major Projects — ambitious year-long undertakings like a new port, university campus or signature landmark
**Mayoral Elections** Every year your city holds a democratic election. Two candidates campaign with distinct specialties and slogans. Endorse your preferred candidate to add your influence but the citizens decide... The winning mayor's specialty influences which proposals citizens bring forward.
**The City Newspaper** After every election, your city gets a newspaper. One of three publications may appear - The Herald, The Uproar, or the Civic Pulse — each with their own style and personality. Papers cover the result, vote shares, turnout, a winner quote, and local filler stories. The game pauses automatically while you read.
**Note** Civic Voice is a roleplay and immersion mod. It reads your city's real data to generate meaningful proposals and elections but does not directly modify gameplay mechanics. Safe to add or remove from any city at any time. Does not conflict with other mods.
Feedback is welcome :)

ChangeLog.Value: v0.4.0 — After every election, your city now gets a newspaper. Three different publications can appear - The Herald, The Uproar, or the Civic Pulse - each with their own style and personality. Papers include the election result, candidate vote shares, turnout, a winner quote, local filler stories, and a splash headline that changes based on how the vote went. The game pauses automatically while you read. Also includes new and expanded city proposals, and various bug fixes.
